story_title: Ted Turner, CNN founder, dies at 87
story_one_liner: Sixteen countries reported the same death yesterday. Five of them told you a different story about who Ted Turner actually was.
voice_style: BBC-broadcaster cadence, slightly slower for an obituary
music_brief: ambient drone bed; intro sting handles open

world_tickers.headline: Japan to buy 20m UAE oil bypassing Hormuz
US trade gap widens on AI imports

scenes.voiceover: Ted Turner died yesterday. Sixteen countries reported it. They told five very different stories.
He launched CNN in 1980. Married Jane Fonda. Donated a billion to the U.N. So how do you tell that story?
scenes.on_screen_text:
FOUNDED CNN
Every country, the same death.

scenes.voiceover: Most of the world ran the wire. Brash sportsman. Media empire. Eight outlets, near-verbatim.
scenes.on_screen_text:
🌐 WIRE
Globe & Mail / Bangkok Post / Al Jazeera
scenes.headline_quoted: The brash sportsman whose ambition led to a media empire.

scenes.voiceover: America's right ran Trump's tribute approvingly.
scenes.headline_quoted: Ted Turner. One of the Greats of All Time. A friend of mine.

scenes.voiceover: But London read Trump's tribute very differently.
scenes.headline_quoted: Trump pays tribute by taking a shot at CNN: 'Destroyed his baby.'

scenes.voiceover: Brazil was the only outlet that named what CNN had actually covered.
scenes.headline_quoted: From the collapse of the Soviet Union to Tiananmen, to reporters in Baghdad under American bombs.

scenes.voiceover: Pakistan made it personal. Geo News led with Turner's father's suicide and the sister he lost to lupus.
scenes.headline_quoted: His father shot himself in 1963. His sister Mary Jean died of lupus. 'What had she done wrong?'

scenes.voiceover: One man. Five very different lives. Could every one of them be telling the truth?
scenes.on_screen_text:
Same death. Five lives.
Daily Framings — follow.

fact_check_provenance.primary_event_source: Turner Enterprises press release via CNN
